sourceMovie.title: Eternals
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eternals works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Avengers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Avengers (2012) is the natural next step after Eternals: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Eternals who respond to its science-fiction will find Avengers: Infinity War (2018) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Eternals fans keep landing on Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 draws from the same science-fiction well as Eternals, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2017 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Thor: Ragnarok
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Thor: Ragnarok (2017) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Eternals later expanded. If Eternals resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Captain Marvel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of Eternals — that tense edge, the action — translates cleanly into Captain Marvel (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Man of Steel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Man of Steel (2013) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Eternals rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Venom: Let There Be Carnage (2021) is the kind of film Eternals fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Predator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predator shares Eternals's gift for science-fiction — the 1987 film delivers action without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Armageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Eternals compelling — the science-fiction, the action — shows up in Armageddon (1998) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eternals?
faq.answer: Try The Avengers, Avengers: Infinity War, Avengers: Age of Ultron—each shares DNA with Eternals while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eternals?
faq.answer: Eternals (2021) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eternals worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
